text:
Water -Water, Fire, Air, Sky, Earth
Fire - Fire, Air, Sky, Earth, Water
Air -Air, Sky, Earth, Water, Fire
Sky -Sky, Earth, Water, Fire, Air
Proportion between each An tara tatva is similar to as told
earlier; such as maximum time for Earth and least for sky.
The rule is that "child would be born either in Air tatva or
Air Antara tatva only". This is a common belief among
astrologers, but there is no quote available that supports the
same. Yajnavalkya smriti says -

;B~ir~ffi~n~·m~e~R~e~ct~ifi~zc~a~ti~on~M~e~th~o~ru~· ______________________4 3

f.l:dl<id <iil1I 'Q<E q;;ffi8Jor~/l
- i!J®q(qi!.J?}fd .
[At the 9th or I Oth month, pushed by strong air (from womb),
with feverish tremble, like a bullet fired, the child comes out,
from mother's womb]
Possibly reference to "Sooti Vayu" (Air push) in the above
sloka and similar one might have helped to strengthen the
belief on "necessity of Air Tatva rise for birth to take place".
